message:
feat(runtime): add inline-mode active widget gauge, scrollback preservation tracing, and 16 unit tests

Introduce a global AtomicU32 gauge (`INLINE_ACTIVE_WIDGETS`) that tracks
the count of live inline-mode TerminalWriter instances, incremented on
construction for Inline/InlineAuto modes and decremented on Drop. This
enables external code to query whether any inline rendering is active
via the newly-exported `inline_active_widgets()` function.

Add scrollback preservation tracing to the inline render path: the
`_inline_span` now records a `scrollback_preserved` field (true on
success, false with a `warn!` diagnostic on failure).

Expand test coverage with 16 new tests covering:
- Gauge increment/decrement correctness for Inline, InlineAuto, and
  AltScreen modes
- Absence of alternate-screen escape sequences (CSI ?1049h/l) in
  inline mode
- Scrollback preservation across renders, resizes, and terminal shrinks
- Multiple coexisting inline writers
- Tracing span emission (`inline.render`)
- No altscreen with scroll-region, hybrid, and mux strategies

Update beads issue tracker: close bd-1q5.15 (inline mode rendering),
move bd-1q5.16 (unit tests for inline mode scrollback) to in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
